channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nigeria Police Redeploy Two DIGs: Achinyan to Force Intelligence Department, Onwuemelie to Logistics and Supply
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force don confirm say dem don redeploy two big oga Deputy Inspectors-General (DIGs) to new positions inside the force. According to internal wireless message wey dem mark as 'Most I...

item.title: Zanzibar uses visitor insurance fees to support health services and visitor care
item.description: Zanzibar government, through the Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar (RGZ), collects around 1 million US dollars monthly from mandatory travel insurance for all visitors. Introduced on October 1, 202...

item.title: Iranian Attacks on Middle East Energy Infrastructure Push Oil Prices Back Above $100
item.description: Oil prices have surged past the $100 per barrel mark due to attacks on Middle East energy infrastructure, mainly attributed to Iran. Brent crude briefly reached $101 after Iranian strikes targeted two...

item.title: Mayon Volcano Records Ash Emission and Lava Fountaining Events, Remains at Alert Level 3
item.description: Mayon Volcano in Albay province exhibited renewed activity yesterday, as reported by the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs). At 4:20 a.m., a brief lava fountaining episode o...

item.title: Dennis Richardson resigns from Bondi massacre inquiry, increasing pressure on royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson, a highly experienced former public servant and former head of ASIO, has resigned from his role investigating security matters linked to the Bondi massacre within the antisemitism ro...

item.title: India Examines Energy Supply Requests from Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Maldives Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: On March 12, 2026, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) of India confirmed that it has received formal requests from Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Maldives for energy supplies, specifically diesel. MEA...

item.title: Suspect killed in vehicle ramming and shooting at Michigan synagogue; no civilian casualties reported
item.description: On 12 March 2026, an armed suspect deliberately drove a truck into Temple Israel, a large Reform Jewish synagogue in West Bloomfield Township, Michigan, which includes an attached preschool and early ...

item.title: PM Modi Discusses West Asia Tensions and Regional Peace with Iranian President
item.description: On 12th March 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a conversation with Iranian President Dr. Masoud Pezeshkian to discuss the growing tensions in West Asia, particularly the ongoing conflict involv...

item.title: Public and private schools in Islamabad observe holiday on March 13
item.description: All public and private educational institutions in Islamabad, including schools, colleges, and universities, will remain closed on March 13, 2026, following a notification issued by the district magis...

item.title: Police Investigation Concludes at Guelph High School Following Lockdown
item.description: Earlier today, a Guelph high school was placed under a temporary lockdown due to a police investigation. Authorities responded promptly to ensure the safety of students and staff, and after thorough c...

item.title: Khaby Lame: From Turin Suburbs to Global TikTok Icon and the Sale of His Digital Identity
item.description: Khabane Lame, better known as Khaby Lame, is the most followed content creator on TikTok, with over 160 million followers. Born in Dakar, Senegal, he moved to Turin, Italy as a child. His fame came du...

item.title: Reflections on Human and Artificial Intelligence
item.description: The article explores the nature of human intelligence in comparison with artificial intelligence (AI). The author argues that AI is often described as merely simulating reasoning, functioning through ...

item.title: Tanzania advances women’s legal rights to own land
item.description: Tanzania has been making important progress in empowering women and girls to legally own land throughout the country. This achievement was highlighted during a side event at the 70th Session of the Co...

item.title: Niagara Regional Chair Bob Gale resigns after controversy over historical collection including Hitler's book
item.description: Bob Gale, the Niagara Regional Chair, resigned abruptly following criticism from local anti-racism groups over his ownership of a signed copy of Adolf Hitler's manifesto, Mein Kampf. The book, purchas...

item.title: Tanzania Explores Growth Opportunities in Carbon Trading Sector
item.description: Stakeholders from government, private sector and development partners highlighted Tanzania's strong potential in carbon trading during a policy dialogue in Dar es Salaam organised by UNCDF through the...

item.title: Twelve House of Representatives Members Defect to APC and ADC, APC Membership Hits 268
item.description: As internal wahala dey affect opposition parties for Nigeria, 12 members for House of Representatives don change their political parties, boosting APC numbers to 268. Six of dem comot from PDP go APC,...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard Issues Navigational Warning for Possible Rocket Debris from China's Long March 8A Launch
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has advised fishermen and vessel crews in the waters around Basilan, Palawan, and Sulu to exercise caution due to potential debris from a Chinese rocket launch schedul...

item.title: Mbeere North Election Petition: Court Reviews Ballot Tampering Allegations
item.description: The High Court in Embu has heard serious claims of ballot box tampering during the Mbeere North parliamentary by-election petition. Julieta Karigi Kithumbu, the petitioner, is challenging the victory ...

item.title: The Trump-Iran conflict expands, drawing multiple countries into regional and global tensions
item.description: Two weeks into military action against Iran, the conflict initiated by the United States and Israel has broadened well beyond its original scope, involving at least 20 countries through missile strike...

item.title: India Condemns Civilian Casualties in US Missile Strike on Iranian School
item.description: India has expressed deep sorrow over the tragic loss of children in a missile attack by the United States on a girls’ school in Iran. The incident, which took place at Shajareh Tayyebeh girls’ element...
